payload.planText:
# Gallery In-Section Navigation (food + property photography)

## Section 1 — Non-Functional Requirements
- Performance: No new dependencies; swipe/keyboard handlers must not add measurable input lag. Lightbox image swap on nav must be instant (browser-cached). Keep loading="lazy" on thumbnails.
- Security: N/A — static client component, no user input persisted, no external calls.
- Reliability: keydown listener attached only while lightbox open and removed on close/unmount (no leaked global listeners). Nav must never produce an out-of-range index (bounded to [0, photos.length-1], no wrap).
- Scalability: Works for any section size (current sections 6–19 photos). No fixed-count assumption.
- Usability: Desktop arrows are keyboard-focusable buttons with aria-label. Esc closes. On first photo prev is hidden; on last, next is hidden. Arrows hidden below md (768px) breakpoint; swipe is the mobile nav. Swipe threshold tuned so vertical scroll/taps are not misread as horizontal swipes.

## Section 2 — Success Metrics
- Functional: In both /foodphotography and /propertyphotography, opening any image and pressing Left/Right (or on-screen chevrons) moves through that section's photos in curated order; Esc and overlay/close button close. On touch, swiping left/right advances within the section. Nav never crosses into another section and stops cleanly at both ends.
- Performance: next build succeeds with no type errors; no console errors/warnings at runtime.
- Quality: Both pages render identically to today except new nav affordances; the two old per-page GalleryGrid.tsx files are removed and both pages import the single shared component.
- Business: Visitors can browse a restaurant/property set fluidly without closing and reopening the lightbox.

## Section 3 — Risks and Assumptions
Assumptions
- Photo src values are unique within a single section (used to map a clicked thumbnail back to its curated index). Verified by inspection of both page.tsx files.
- app/components/ is the home for shared components (confirmed: Footer.tsx lives there; both pages import ../components/Footer).
- Only the food and property pages consume GalleryGrid (confirmed by import inspection).
- No engineering-docs scaffold exists, so no docs task included; consolidating two identical components into one shared file is a minor structural change.

Risks
- Swipe misfires (tap or vertical scroll read as swipe). Mitigation: require horizontal delta >= ~50px AND |dx| > |dy|; ignore otherwise.
- Curated vs column order mismatch. Mitigation: pass flat curated photos array as a new prop; resolve index by reference-then-src.
- forcedRatio object cloning breaks reference equality. Mitigation: fall back to src match (overrides preserve src).
- Stale/leaked keydown listener. Mitigation: effect keyed on open state with cleanup.

## Section 3.5 — Context Package
- app/components/Footer.tsx — shared-component conventions (default export, "use client", styling).
- app/foodphotography/GalleryGrid.tsx and app/propertyphotography/GalleryGrid.tsx — identical components to be replaced.
- app/foodphotography/page.tsx (line 144 call site) and app/propertyphotography/page.tsx (line 186 call site) — call sites + local Photo interface / buildColumns.
- app/foodphotography/galleryColumns.ts — Photo type source (property page defines Photo inline; both are { src; alt; forcedRatio? }).

## Section 4 — Tasks

- [ ] Create shared app/components/GalleryGrid.tsx with in-section navigation
  - Given the two existing per-page GalleryGrid.tsx files are byte-identical and each render a single-image lightbox, When I create one shared client component at app/components/GalleryGrid.tsx accepting props { columns: Photo[][]; photos: Photo[] }, Then it renders the same column grid, tracks activeIndex (number|null) into the flat curated photos, resolves a clicked thumbnail to its curated index (reference match, src fallback), and shows photos[activeIndex] in the modal.
  - Include: desktop prev/next as minimal bare chevrons (semi-transparent white, left/right edges, no background), wrapped hidden md:flex (desktop-only); prev hidden at index 0, next hidden at last index; existing close button + overlay-click close preserved.
  - Include: useEffect (active only while activeIndex !== null) binding keydown for ArrowLeft (prev), ArrowRight (next), Escape (close), with cleanup.
  - Include: touch swipe on the modal — onTouchStart/onTouchEnd computing dx/dy; advance prev/next only when |dx| >= 50px and |dx| > |dy|; no swipe-down-to-close.
  - Nav helpers clamp to [0, photos.length-1] — no wrap-around, never leave the section.
  - NFRs: See Section 1 — listener cleanup, bounded index, no new deps.
  - Verification Gate: npm run build passes with no type errors; Photo type imported/declared consistently.
  - Checkpoint: Commit before starting the next task — do not batch commits across tasks.

- [ ] Wire app/foodphotography/page.tsx to the shared component
  - Given the new shared component exists, When I change the import to ../components/GalleryGrid and pass columns={buildColumns(restaurant.photos, restaurant.layout)} and photos={restaurant.photos}, Then the food gallery renders unchanged and the lightbox navigates in curated order within each restaurant.
  - NFRs: See Section 1.
  - Verification Gate: npm run dev, open /foodphotography: click an image, verify Left/Right + chevrons move through that restaurant only and stop at ends; Esc/close/overlay close; build still passes.
  - Checkpoint: Commit before starting the next task — do not batch commits across tasks.

- [ ] Wire app/propertyphotography/page.tsx to the shared component
  - Given the shared component exists, When I change the import to ../components/GalleryGrid and pass columns={buildColumns(property.photos)} and photos={property.photos} (property keeps its own inline buildColumns), Then the property gallery renders unchanged and navigates in curated order within each property.
  - NFRs: See Section 1.
  - Verification Gate: npm run dev, open /propertyphotography: same nav checks as food; verify the 8-photo default-layout section (with the forcedRatio clone) still maps clicks to the correct curated index; build passes.
  - Checkpoint: Commit before starting the next task — do not batch commits across tasks.

- [ ] Delete the two obsolete per-page GalleryGrid.tsx files and verify mobile swipe
  - Given both pages now import the shared component, When I delete app/foodphotography/GalleryGrid.tsx and app/propertyphotography/GalleryGrid.tsx, Then no import resolves to the deleted files and the app still builds.
  - Manually verify swipe via browser device emulation (or real device): left/right swipe advances within a section; vertical scroll and taps do not trigger nav; arrows hidden below md.
  - NFRs: See Section 1.
  - Verification Gate: npm run build passes; grep finds no remaining import of the deleted paths; mobile-emulated swipe works on both routes.
  - Checkpoint: Commit before finishing — do not batch commits across tasks.

## Section 5 — Definition of Done
- [ ] All tasks above are implemented and committed
- [ ] Each task was verified and committed before the next began — no batch commits
- [ ] Verification gate passed for every task before proceeding
- [ ] No LOAD-BEARING task was skipped or deferred
- [ ] Plan mode used for any LOAD-BEARING task
- [ ] Acceptance criteria pass for every task (desktop arrows + keyboard, mobile swipe, curated order, stop-at-ends, single section scope)
- [ ] All documentation/ADR tasks from Section 4 are implemented (none in this plan)
- [ ] NFR benchmarks verified (no leaked listeners, bounded index, no new deps, next build clean)
- [ ] No critical or high-severity bugs open
- [ ] Peer code review completed
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline passing (build, lint, tests)
